author: @Barbreck1
text:
The 'Theory of Gravity', which begins with a revision of our understanding of the atom...
Consider, as a starting point, the Hydrogen atom which has one electron trapped in an orbital around the nucleus.  It is this physical arrangement that gives us the most fundamental assembly that we identify as 'an atom'.  
The Standard Model likes to give the effects involved in maintaining this assembly obscure terms like the 'weak nuclear force' and 'strong nuclear force' but fails to identify what these "forces" are.
Iposit that the strong nuclear force is an electrostatic effect- and I shall defend that position with evidence.  I also posit that the weak nuclear force is not a force at all but instead the effect of the centripetal acceleration (centrifugal force) generated by the electron's velocity whilst trapped in the orbital.
Consider:
We know very well that when electrons are conducted in an orbital pathway- such as around a conductive coil- they generate an electromagnetic force.  It is my position that the electrons in atoms are doing exactly the same thing while in orbit around an atomic nucleus. 
However, please bear in mind that we cannot refer to this force as  "electromagnetic" as the electrons are not travelling through, nor exerting a force upon, conductive materials.  Instead, the electrons are  trapped around the atomic nucleus and are (due to their motion) exerting an attractive effect upon surrounding electrons in other atoms.  They are 'electrostatic', (electrons that are moving but not travelling).
And yes, this is exactly the same force that holds a balloon to a non-conductive surface like a wall after you've rubbed it on your t-shirt! 
This model answers several other questions about gravity and particularly in material cohesion, in that: 
a) The electrostatic attractive force (aka strong force) of the moving electron pulls it into orbit around the nucleus at extreme velocity.  As a result, it experiences an opposing centripetal effect (the weak nuclear force) that prevents the electron from falling into the nucleus.  
b) Materials (collections of atoms) bind strongly together as a result of the same electrostatic effect interacting between atoms (the electrons of each atom are effectively attracting the electrons of their neighbouring atoms).  The more electrons the atoms collectively have, the denser the material (more electrons attracting more electrons).  This runs in accordance with the electron counts in the periodic table.
(Experimental proof:  When we chill a material to absolute zero, we slow down and stop the electron orbitals.  This causes materials to crumble, because the electrostatic binding has been neutralised.  Certain materials can also self-levitate- they effectively become weightless or "gravitationally resistant"). 
c) Gravity is the collective electrostatic effect of all of the electrons present in a given mass of atoms.  The greater the number of atoms (and consequently electrons), the greater the resultant mass and gravitational potential. 
This effect is multiplied and effectively "transmitted" atom-to-atom throughout all of the atoms in physical contact with the rest of the mass 
If we bear in mind that space, while effectively a vacuum, is still awash with a tenuous aether of atoms and electrons- mostly due to the stellar wind generated constantly by our host star, then gravity is transmitted as far as any atoms or free electrons that may be present.  Of course, the greater the density of the atoms and electrons, the greater the gravitational potential. 
The collective stellar body of the galaxy is exerting it's influence upon all the other masses within the collective plasma bubble of the galaxies' stellar emission.  This explains Galactic structure.  It also eliminates any requirement for black holes at the centre of each galaxy. 
Conclusion:
Gravity is an electrostatic effect between the electrons of atoms which can only be neutralised by the elimination of electron spin through extreme chilling or by utilising/inventing physical materials that are not dependent upon electron-carrying atoms.
Thanks.

author: @russianbot6968
text:
Similar conclusion can be drawn about time, as you draw about energy and gravity. Concept of time is useful, but when digging down into what it is, all one can point to when describing "time" is it's effects on systems it interacts with.

I am of the opinion that time is just the process of motion of matter. You take away the motion and matter is effectively "frozen in time".

Continuing on this train of thought, it would seem that time, gravity and energy are linked to motion and that it might be the fundamental thing that underlines the three concepts.

Would recommend looking into lectures by Carlo Rovelli, a physicist that seems to have similar ponderings on the issue.

author: @garybarbourii8274
text:
+Robert Murray-Smith
I have been watching your videos on supercapacitors for the last week. I would very much like to speak with you on that topic, but I intend to study a bit more before asking your precious time on that matter. 

However, this morning I came across your talk on Gravity and got excited. It is wildly coincidental that just last night it sunk in to me that the relativistic speeds with which electrons travel on graphene could be the ideal nano-substitute I've been contemplating to replace the large vacuum chambers and high voltage accelerators that I had originally envisioned for my own experiment on gravity. I had written off a nano-solution as impractical for my DIY capabilities until now. As it turns out graphene and gravity may have a whole lot to do with each other in our future technology (graphene sheets conducting electricity while laid at a particular angle to a gravitational field may adequately substitute the target "range of velocities" mentioned in the last paragraph, yielding a bidirectional energy transfer between electricity and a gravitational "field").

below are some excerpts from my pitch that apparently did not meet the criteria for a kickstarter project. I call it Superasymmetry. If you would like to discuss anything, my email is g2barbour@gmail.com. I think you'll find the concept of gravity being an effect of other forces and effects, and not an independent force of its own, particularly interesting (forgive me if my math is not up to par, I have no calculus, nor am I a physicist by trade):

Imagine a world where we could harness the force of gravity as easily electricity and magnetism. Nothing would have a greater impact on our understanding of the universe. The advancements would be mind-blowing. Yet, it has eluded us through a revolution in technology.  

We have been looking at it all wrong...  

I have an idea that might just lead us to the answer, and there is an experiment that will tell us.  

If it works:  

Anti-gravity  

Abundant clean energy 

Grand unification

Elimination of the gravitational constant and the awkward need for dark matter/energy  

Insight into an expanding universe, quantum vacuum fluctuations, our concept of mass, the relationship between electric and magnetic fields, and between the strong and weak nuclear forces  

Unimaginable technology, discovery, and insight into existence: the world becomes more complete, more elegant  

Concept: 

Perhaps Einstein had completed his lifes work after all, and the full implications of relativity describe both gravity, and the so-called hidden dimensions that modern theory uses to explain it. Maybe relativity IS the hidden dimensions. (Gravity may be an artifact of asymmetric relativistic effects rather than an independent force of its own)  

To explain, lets start by accelerating something to the speed of light. According to modern interpretation, our ability to speed it up diminishes to zero as it reaches the speed of light (because its mass becomes infinite).  

Here's what matters: the greater the difference in velocity between things, the less their ability to interact. Since we are already familiar with spatial distance between objects (inches, miles, etc), we will call this difference in velocity a "perceptive" distance. And because a force between objects diminishes at a greater distance, we get the same effect by increasing the velocity between things as by moving them further apart. (It would be these perceptive distances both toward and away from an object in each of the 3 spatial dimensions that are mathematically described as 6 "hidden" dimensions of modern theory)  

Example: 

There is a proton and electron, very close to each other, moving in the same direction away from a second electron. The proton is moving slower than the electron it is near, but we've chosen an instant in time where they are both the same distance from the second electron. (The proton is close enough to its nearby electron that, at their distance from the second electron, assuming they occupy the same space wont really throw our numbers or be unrealistic)  

The proton is moving away from the second electron at 4.5%C (C is the speed of light), and the two electron are moving away from each other at 6%C.  

If velocity is effectively a perceptive distance ranging from zero to infinity, we can use the formula: Sqrt(1-(V/C)2) to tell us how much affect remains between two particles after accounting for their perceptive distance. 

Doing the math: The proton attracts the second electron with .9989869869 times the vigor of stationary particles at the same distance, while the electrons only retain .9981983771 of their ability to repel each other. That leaves us with a net attraction of .0007886098, or about 1/1268 the electric force between a proton and electron at the same distance. (Even though the second electron is the same distance from the proton and the first electron, there is a relativistic asymmetry in their affect on each other) 

This example shows what it means for space to be asymmetric (or "curved," which is the traditional metaphor for gravity), but this concept is more dynamic, and whether "curvature" appears to be attraction, repulsion, or nothing at all is a matter of perspective. 

The experiment to test this is straightforward: Send a stream of electrons up away from the earths gravity through a range of velocities while looking for small inconsistencies in the energy they carry at the end of their journey. The idea is that above a certain speed the electrons will be less attracted to earth, then repelled by it, and that we can measure these tiny effects. (Success will show a fairly linear output through the range of velocities consistent with the thermal and nuclear activity of the earths mass, then a gradual decline in gravitational attraction, peaking at repulsion somewhere near the range of energy reflected by the orbital velocities of the earths electrons, then tapering off again. The experiment may prove relativistic asymmetry, and give us a crude manipulation of gravity, but much work will remain to true understanding. I suspect gravity compounds and manifests at multiple scales to create the effect we have hoisted so precariously upon the gravitational constant)

author: @Unifrog_
text:
I don't see a contradiction with gravity making matter more ordered and entropy because entropy is about the number of possible states a system can be in (there are usually way more possible disordered states than disordered states so usually a system will tend towards disorder simply as a matter of probability) with gravity though the number of states in the system that can result is not just a function of the position of matter, there is also all the energy in the system which also has state and when matter orders itself due to gravity potential energy is converted into heat / radiation. all of that radiation will spread outward in ways that are far more complex than the decrease in entropy of the matter rather than being constrained as a state of the matter so order is not actually increasing due to gravity when you view the system as a whole (although you might wonder where the potential energy that drives all this came from or how the system was so ordered to begin with)

If you had a universe that had way more ordered states than disordered ones due to some artificial constraint (I think you are correct in pointing out that order is just a label that humans make but it also has an objective meaning in that to order something is to impose constraints on it which by definition reduce the number of possibilities that system could be in) entropy would actually drive such a system from disorder to order.

I think a good way to think about the use of energy in moving a system from disorder to order is not the transition from the disordered state to the ordered state as that could happen without the use of any energy whatsoever but its the application of constraints on the evolution of your system to eliminate all those future disordered states which is where you are expending your energy. 

If you could figure out ways to change the evolution of a system to what you want while limiting the number of constraints you are imposing on it then that seems like a good guide to increasing efficiency beyond simply using energy from one system to lower the entropy of another.

author: @DigGil3
text:
The best explanation of gravity I know of, in the light of Relativity: https://youtu.be/jlTVIMOix3I

You'd be interested to know that equation for gravity/time also seem to work to derive equations for Electromagnetism (read more: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gravitoelectromagnetism), so there's something important going on there. Unfortunately, Gravity seems like being an attractive only kind of thing while electromagnetism is not.

It is my understanding that our 3-dimensional world lies at the surface of a 4-D Universe which somehow can be distorted by mass. In the 4-D perspective objects tend to compress together (as in a fabric being pinched), but in our 3-D view we perceive this a spatial approach between bodies, ergo motion. This "somehow can be distorted" is what the Higgs Theory could unveil. There are many questions to be answered, but I want to keep this post short.

author: @DrSaminstine
text: Part1 Electricity passing through a conductor induces a magnetic field a magnetic field rotating next to a conductor induces electricity. Induction motors use a rotating magnetic field to spin a conductor, the induced fields interact and induce eddy currents that disrupt electrical and magnetic flows by opposing fields inducing heat or "inferred" energy as the fields and flows collide. The earth is a giant conductor spinning in a much larger magnetic field forming its own magnetic field in opposition and we see eddy currents form in the form of precipitation note the spiral patterns formed as the high frequency static super charges the largest conductor on the planet (salt water =water +electrolyte) H2O atoms begin to spin as the static charge from the ion tubes at the poles build capacitance in the salt water resistance eddy currents in water atoms induce heat and show up over oceans as inferred charged particles start to flow to the path of least resistance (storms move land ward) charged particles find ground and discharge (lightning) particles polarize attract and combine forming rain could the heating and cooling be just a byproduct of the larger system where static electricity is the hidden answer to a grand answer electrostatic is also present in the atomic structure as well as bonds think about a static charged ballon sticking to a wall is it defying gravity or showing us something about everything just some of my thoughts 😊

author: @erlendse
text:
Not so sure I agree in gravity being movement;
but it sure can lead to movement, and keep large objects circulating around together with other big objects.

You are right about schoolbook being wrong, due to simplification. Like gravity and a flat plane, good luck finding the flat gravity plane.(would require a solid slab of infinite size) Even the flat sea of the ocean is curved.

Most of the mistake starts in the simplification, like the rock being moved against gravity and the energy "recovered" by letting a massive block of mass attract(likely the same you did drag it away from) it and extracting the force of the movement.

The model is kinda flawed; if you get the rock into space, and let it be dragged in by another planet, you could possibly get more energy than it takes to remove it from earth.(not counting in that massive rocket) Where does that energy come from? Messed up "zero point" in one of the pages?

My question would be: Why would something have less "energy" by being stuck to something else? Is rock in space high "energy", even with lack of gravity?

A system without movement in any form can't be observed, since observing require movement. Energy is something that is used/gained/transformed when a movement change.

This is probably a lot of non-sense, but still what's your take on it?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: erlendse It's an argument about cause and effect. A which came first argument - the chicken or the egg. Yet we know they arose together. So, I think you slightly got hold of the wrong end of the stick - I am not saying gravity causes motion. Motion is gravity - there is no cause and effect here.

replies.author: @erlendse
replies.text:
Chicken and egg yes.. chickens rarely lay eggs.. mostly older ones do.

How it all started? Maybe it didn't?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: erlendse indeed

author: @explosu
text:
There is math governing the pattern of any gait, ordered by the brain to maintain movement. You're talking about gravity as a translation from disorder to order - if there is no particle or any sort of manipulable force behind gravity, do you think it's a huge leap to say that gravity is basically just a sorting algorithm applied to every point of mass? As a programmer, if I were to implement gravity, I wouldn't use a particle system, or any kind of system for gravity itself - I would simply check each point in the system that has mass and apply it as a single function.

Also, I just want to say I love your videos. I don't always understand what you're doing but it always ends up leading to that point. You're a great teacher :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: explosu Maths doesn't govern anything mate - maths is just a language we use to describe things - and i am not talking about gravity as having a direction - i point out an interesting point arising from conceptions of gravity in terms of order and disorder - but my point about gravity is that gravity is an emergent property of real systems in relation with movement through time and space and there i think we agree.

author: @trailkeeper
text:
some thoughts I had on this subject:
There is also still a mystery to magnetism, and other forces, and they conveniently describe them as "lines of force", but that is only the effect of magnetism, and they
don't know what is in that invisible field/area. Due to the alignment of the atoms/electrons in a magnet, it is as if the apparent mass has increased in one
direction, and hence it's like gravity increases in that direction which causes the attraction.  But unlike gravity which is equally available for any material with the same
mass,  the material being affected by must be capable of being magnetized itself (even if its just temporary like for copper coils, etc).  The result is that these forces may be not only space and time dependent/relative, but directional.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: trailkeeper maybe

replies.author: @trailkeeper
replies.text:
Robert Murray-Smith There could be a "spring analogy" possibility into all of this:

Gravity or magnetism is like something two masses on a spring.  The spring can be compressed, which will want to cause a repulsion, and the spring can be stretched which will cause an attraction.  If one mass is bigger than the other, it will not move as much due to inertia.

author: @VICTORIA-SAYERS
text:
Great Video Robert, I Love Talking about Energy and Gravity, my Personal View of Energy is that there are two Types of Energy, Intelligent and Non-Intelligent Energy.

In other Words, what we can see in the Universe is the Result of Non-Intelligent Energy, Idle Energy, like Cosmic Rays, Gamma Rays, X-Rays, Far and Near Infrared Rays, Ultra Violet Rays, ect, but what we can not see (well, for the most Part) is the Intelligent Energy that is the Result of the Spirit within every Living Creature, Energy that has a Mind, Will and Emotions of it's own. Intelligent Energy is what Moves Non-Intelligent Energy throughout the Universe.
